elements.sent-2411-001: Parton And-hadron China Institute of Atomic Energy (PACIAE) is a multipurpose Monte Carlo event generator developed to describe a wide range of high-energy collisions, including lepton-lepton, lepton-hadron, lepton-nucleus, hadron-hadron, hadron-nucleus, and nucleus-nucleus collisions.
elements.sent-2411-002: It is built based on the PYTHIA program, and incorporates parton and hadron cascades to address the nuclear medium effects.
elements.sent-2411-003: PACIAE 4.0 is the new generation of PACIAE model surpassing the version 3.0.
elements.sent-2411-004: In PACIAE 4.0, the old fixed-format FORTRAN 77 code has been refactored and rewritten by the free-format modern Fortran and C++ languages.
elements.sent-2411-005: The C++-based PYTHIA 8.3 is interfaced in, while previous versions connected to the Fortran-based PYTHIA 6.4 only.
elements.sent-2411-006: Several improvements are also introduced, which enable PACIAE 4.0 to contain more physics and features to model the high-energy collisions.
elements.sent-2411-007: This is the first attempt to transition PACIAE from Fortran to C++.
elements.sent-2411-008: MC models are able to give a fairly good description of the basic experimental observables.
elements.sent-2411-009: However, as more and more experimental data become available, more accurate modeling is required.
elements.sent-2411-010: PYTHIA has been upgraded to the C++-based PYTHIA 8.3~[9] with more physics and features.
elements.sent-2411-011: Therefore we upgrade the PACIAE model to the new version of PACIAE 4.0 with the option to either PYTHIA 6.4~[8] or PYTHIA 8.3~[9].
elements.sent-2411-012: In addition, several improvements have been introduced in this new version.
elements.sent-2411-013: The running time is 1–1000 events per minute, depending on the collisions system studied.. \end{small.}
elements.sent-2411-014: PACIAE is a parton and hadron cascade model to simulate the high-energy collisions.
elements.sent-2411-015: It stems from the LUnd and CIAE (LUCIAE) Monte Carlo program~\cite{Andersson:1996xi,Sa:1995fj,Tai:1998hc , which connected to FRITIOF 7~\cite{Pi:1992ug,Andersson:1992iq , JETSET 7~\cite{Sjostrand:1993yb , PYTHIA 5~\cite{Sjostrand:1993yb and ARIADNE 4~\cite{Lonnblad:1992tz .}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: LUCIAE implemented both the Firecracker Model (FCM) for the collective multigluon emission from the color fields of interacting strings and the reinteraction of the final state hadrons to study the collective and the rescattering effects.
elements.sent-2411-017: After that, the JETSET and PYTHIA CIAE (JPCIAE)~\cite{Sa:2001ma was developed and detached FRITIOF and ARIADNE.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Soon after, JETSET had been integrated into PYTHIA 6.1~\cite{Sjostrand:2000wi .}
elements.sent-2411-019: JPCIAE was renamed to PACIAE 1.0~\cite{Sa:2004zc,Sa:2006zf,Zhou:2006ska accordingly, and took the parton rescattering into account.}
elements.sent-2411-020: In 2012, the first official version PACIAE 2.0~\cite{Sa:2011ye was released and then updated to version 2.1~\cite{Sa:2012hb , 2.2~\cite{Zhou:2014qpa,Yan:2018mpp,She:2022vco,Yan:2022byw and 3.0~\cite{Lei:2023srp over the past decade.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: However, in the previous PACIAE 2 series~\cite{Sa:2011ye,Sa:2012hb,Zhou:2014qpa,Yan:2018mpp,She:2022vco,Yan:2022byw and 3.0~\cite{Lei:2023srp , the core component PYTHIA was restricted to the version 6.4~\cite{Sjostrand:2006za , although it has been upgraded to the version 8~\cite{Sjostrand:2007gs,Sjostrand:2014zea,Bierlich:2022pfr .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: A historical reason is that the older PACIAE and PYTHIA 6 were written with the process-oriented FORTRAN77 programming language, while PYTHIA 8 has embraced the objective-oriented C++.
elements.sent-2411-023: On the other hand, as recent experimental data and theoretical studies suggested~\cite{Weber:2018ddv,ALICE:2020msa,Vermunt:2019ecg,ALICE:2020wfu,ALICE:2021npz,Bierlich:2023okq , more physical mechanisms, e.g. multiparton interactions (MPI), color reconnections (CR), etc. and the more accurate modeling are required.}
elements.sent-2411-024: Therefore, we upgrade PACIAE 2 series and PACIAE 3.0 to the version 4.0.
elements.sent-2411-025: In this version, the program is rewritten by the modern Fortran and C++ languages.
elements.sent-2411-026: Now PACIAE 4.0 is a Fortran-C++ mixing language program and is able to connect to the C++-based PYTHIA 8.
elements.sent-2411-027: At the same time, the excellent PYTHIA 6 is also retained to provide some convenient uses, such as the independent fragmentation (IF).
elements.sent-2411-028: The code structure of PACIAE 4.0 has been refactored and redesigned, which gives the possibility to use almost all features of PYTHIA.
elements.sent-2411-029: The mass-corrected cross sections of the heavy quarks related to parton-parton scatterings are introduced in the partonic rescattering stage.
elements.sent-2411-030: The coalescence hadronization model is improved, and additional hadron-hadron reaction channels are incorporated into the hadronic rescattering stage.
elements.sent-2411-031: PACIAE 4.0 program is now hosted on the open source platforms GitHub~\footnote{https://github.com/ArcsaberHep/PACIAE4 and Gitee~\footnote{https://gitee.com/arcsaberhep/PACIAE4 .}}
elements.sent-2411-032: The program flow of PACIAE 4.0 is shown in Fig.~\ref{fig:program_flow .}
elements.sent-2411-033: In general, a full parton and hadron cascade simulation of PACIAE 4.0 comprise of four stages: initial state, parton cascade (rescattering), hadronization, and hadron cascade (rescattering).
elements.sent-2411-034: The skeleton of PACIAE 4.0 is still written in Fortran language.
elements.sent-2411-035: A Fortran main program steers the whole execution of PACIAE.
elements.sent-2411-036: At the beginning and the end, the main program instantiates and deletes PYTHIA 8 objects on the heap, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-037: For the initial state, PYTHIA 8 provides the generation of hard processes, the construction of strings, etc. At the hadronization stage, if the Lund string fragmentation regime was selected, PYTHIA 8 will handle it and fragments parton strings to colorless hadrons.
elements.sent-2411-038: In these procedures, PACIAE interacts with PYTHIA 8 via the Fortran-C++ interface.
elements.sent-2411-039: Thanks to the INTERFACE block of the modern Fortran, it is easy to realize.
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics[width=1\textwidth]{program_flow.eps} \caption{The program flow of PACIAE 4.0 when the PYTHIA 8 is chosen. PACIAE interacts with C++-based PYTHIA 8 via the interface module.} \label{fig:program_flow}
elements.sent-2411-040: The basic simulation frameworks are A-, B- and C-framework, corresponding to the low-energy ($\sqrt{s_{NN < 3 $ GeV) simulation, the high-energy ($\sqrt{s_{NN \ge 3 $ GeV) hadronic simulation and the high-energy parton and hadron cascade simulation inherited from PACIAE 3.0~\cite{Lei:2023srp .}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-041: In PACIAE 4.0, according to the different initial state provider, we design several different frameworks (modes):.
elements.sent-2411-042: In the following sections, we will focus on the parton and hadron cascade process.
elements.sent-2411-043: For the purely hadronic simulation in PACIAE, we refer to Ref.~\cite{Lei:2023srp for more details.}
elements.sent-2411-044: The parameters and switches controlling the simulation can be divided into two categories: PACIAE-related and PYTHIA-related.
elements.sent-2411-045: In the previous versions of PACIAE, all of the input parameters, both PACIAE-related and PYTHIA-related, were set in a single ``usu.dat'' file.
elements.sent-2411-046: In PACIAE 4.0, we decouple these two sets into an input file ``usu.dat'' for the main PACIAE-related parameters and two extra input files ``pythia6\_extra.cfg'' and ``pythia8\_extra.cfg'' for the PYTHIA6/8-related parameters \footnote{ To use the ``pythia6\_extra.cfg'' and ``pythia8\_extra.cfg'', the knowledge of PYTHIA parameters and switches is required.}
elements.sent-2411-047: Important references include: (1) \href{https://doi.org/10.1088/1126-6708/2006/05/026 {PYTHIA 6.4 Physics and Manual, JHEP 05 (2006) 026 ; (2) \href{https://pythia.org/download/pythia6/pythia6428.update {PYTHIA 6.4 Update Notes ; (3) \href{https://pythia.org//latest-manual/Welcome.html {PYTHIA 8 Online Manual, https://pythia.org//latest-manual/Welcome.html .. .}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-048: Essentially, ``pythia6\_extra.cfg'' and ``pythia8\_extra.cfg'' files are the ``card files'' of PYTHIA 6 and 8~\cite{Sjostrand:2006za,Bierlich:2022pfr .}
elements.sent-2411-049: They allow more flexibility and freedom in the use of PYTHIA 6 and 8.
elements.sent-2411-050: A proton-proton ($ pp $) pair is firstly executed by PYTHIA 8.
elements.sent-2411-051: PYTHIA 8 generates the hard processes at the leading order (LO), the space-like initial-state radiation (ISR) and the time-like final-state radiation (FSR) with the parton shower approach, as well as the MPI in the interleaved evolution~\cite{Bierlich:2022pfr,Corke:2010yf .}
elements.sent-2411-052: The strings from PYTHIA including hard, semi-hard, soft partons and the beam remnants (diquarks mainly) are extracted.
elements.sent-2411-053: Then the strings and diquarks are forced to break up into free partons to obtain the initial partonic state of the $ pp $ collision.
elements.sent-2411-054: The positions of nucleons inside a nucleus $ A $ are sampled with the Woods-Saxon distribution \begin{equation}\rho(r) = \rho_0 [ 1 + \exp{ ( \frac{ r - R }{d} ) } ]^{-1}, \label{eq:woods_saxon}\end{equation} where $ r $ is the distance between a nucleon and the center of the nucleus, $ R $ is the classical radius of the nucleus, and $ d \approx 0.54 $ fm is the nuclear depth.
elements.sent-2411-055: The normalization constant $ \rho_0 $ reads \begin{equation}\rho_0 = \frac{ A }{ \frac{ 4 \pi }{ 3 } R^3 }. \label{eq:woods_saxon_norm_rho0}\end{equation} Here we use $ A $ denoting both the nucleus and its atomic number.
elements.sent-2411-056: The beam direction is set along $ z $ axis.
elements.sent-2411-057: For $ pA $ and $ AB $ collisions, the projectile and the target are positioned at $ ( +\frac{b {2 , 0, -20 )$ and $ ( -\frac{b {2 , 0, +20 )$ (in units of fm), respectively.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-058: The impact parameter angle (event-plane angle) $ \phi_{ b $ is fixed to 0.}
elements.sent-2411-059: In PACIAE 4.0, the upper limit of the nucleus radius is set as 10 fm $ > R$, which means the maximum impact parameter will be 20 fm, similar to the Monte Carlo Glauber (MCG) model~\cite{Alver:2008aq,Loizides:2014vua,Loizides:2017ack .}
elements.sent-2411-060: It enables us to capture the nucleon fluctuation in the peripheral collisions.
elements.sent-2411-061: The time origin is set to the moment when the first nucleon-nucleon ($ NN $) collides.
elements.sent-2411-062: Due to the very high collision energy, the Fermi motion of nucleons is neglected, i.e., the transverse momenta $ p_x = p_y = 0 $.
elements.sent-2411-063: The longitudinal momentum $ p_z $ and the energy $E$ of nucleons will be assigned according to the required collision energy.
elements.sent-2411-064: Two nucleons will collide if their minimum distance $ d_{min $ satisfies \begin{equation}d_{min} \le \sqrt{ \frac{ \sigma^{NN}_{tot} }{ \pi } }, \label{eq:d_min}\end{equation} where $ \sigma^{NN _{tot $ is the total $ NN $ cross section in units of fm$^2$ and $ d_{min $ is defined in the center-of-mass (CM) frame of the two colliding nucleons.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-065: This criterion is also applied to the partonic and hadronic rescattering stages, just with different particle species and total cross sections.
elements.sent-2411-066: The $ \sigma^{NN _{tot $ is calculated as the sum of the pomeron and the reggeon terms using the widely-accepted Donnachie-Landshoff (DL) parameterization~\cite{Donnachie:1992ny \begin{equation}\sigma^{NN}_{\mathrm{tot}}(s) = X s^{\epsilon} + Y s^{-\eta} ~, \label{pg:sigtotpomreg}\end{equation} where $ s $ is the squared CM energy.}}}
elements.sent-2411-068: The coefficients $ X $ and $ Y $ are 21.7 and 56.08, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-069: Each colliding $ NN $ pair will be executed by PYTHIA 8, and free partons are obtained in the manner described in Sec.~\ref{subsec:pp_collisions .}
elements.sent-2411-070: These partons compose the initial partonic state of a $ pA $ or $ AB $ collision.
elements.sent-2411-071: With the upgrade of PYTHIA, the heavy-ion simulations, the Angantyr model~\cite{Bierlich:2018xfw , has been developed and integrated into the standard PYTHIA 8 package~\cite{Bierlich:2022pfr .}}
elements.sent-2411-072: Angantyr is inspired by the old FRITIOF model~\cite{Pi:1992ug,Andersson:1992iq,Nilsson-Almqvist:1986ast and inherits the name from one of its subroutines directly.}
elements.sent-2411-073: Compared to the standard Glauber formalism, Angantyr implements the more advanced GLISSANDO model~\cite{Broniowski:2007nz,Rybczynski:2013yba and the DoubleStrikman model~\cite{Alvioli:2013vk , in which the elaborate color fluctuations inside nucleons are coded.}}
elements.sent-2411-074: Recent studies~\cite{Zheng:2021jrr,Zheng:2024xyv have shown such a substructure might be of importance to the collectivity of the small systems.}
elements.sent-2411-075: In PACIAE 4.0, it is able to generate an Angantyr initial partonic state following the same way in Sec.~\ref{subsec:pp_collisions , then push the parton reservoir into the parton cascade evolution.}
elements.sent-2411-076: While the aforementioned initial states consist of the relatively large number of jet partons, PACIAE could provide another special initial state: deexcited quarks-antiquarks state.
elements.sent-2411-077: In this special initial state, there are only quarks and antiquarks.
elements.sent-2411-078: The gluons are converted to quark-antiquark ($q \bar{q}$) pairs, and the energetic quarks/antiquarks excite $q \bar{q}$ pairs from the vacuum.
elements.sent-2411-079: The details are described in Sec.~\ref{subsubsec:coalescence .}
elements.sent-2411-080: Since PACIAE is built based on the PYTHIA program, in addition to the basic $ pp / pA / AB $, it can accept any beams supported by PYTHIA 6/8, including but not limited to the electron $ e^{- $, electron neutrino $ \nu_e $, photon $ \gamma $, pion $ \pi^+ $, antiproton $ \bar{p $, $ \Lambda $ hyperon, etc. We refer to Refs.~\cite{Zhou:2014qpa,Sjostrand:2006za,Bierlich:2022pfr for more information.}}}
elements.sent-2411-081: The possible signals for the formation of the hot and dense nuclear matter, quark gluon plasma (QGP), have been detected by the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC)~\cite{BRAHMS:2004adc,PHOBOS:2004zne,STAR:2005gfr,PHENIX:2004vcz and the Large Hadron Collider (LHC)~\cite{ALICE:2010suc,ALICE:2010yje,ATLAS:2010isq,CMS:2011iwn .}}
elements.sent-2411-082: To study such a unique QCD matter, it is inevitable to model the interactions between deconfined partons.
elements.sent-2411-083: The Monte Carlo technique is a very effective method~\cite{Geiger:1991nj , and there are copious parton transport models in the market, such as ZPC (AMPT)~\cite{Zhang:1997ej,Lin:2004en , PCPC~\cite{Borchers:2000wf,Nara:2023vrq BAMPS~\cite{Xu:2004mz , PHSD~\cite{Cassing:2009vt , MARTINI~\cite{Schenke:2009gb , PACIAE~\cite{Sa:2011ye , ALPACA~\cite{Kurkela:2022qhn , etc. In PACIAE, we consider only $ 2 \rightarrow 2 $ parton-parton scatterings ( $ a b \rightarrow c d $ ) with the leading-order perturbative quantum chromodynamics (LO-pQCD) cross sections~\cite{Combridge:1977dm .}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-084: The differential cross section reads \begin{equation}\frac{ d\sigma }{ dt } ( ab \rightarrow cd; s, t ) = \frac{ \pi \alpha_s^2 }{ s^2 } | \overline{ \mathcal{ M } } |^2,\end{equation} where $ s $ and $ t $ are squared CM energy and squared momentum transfer, respectively.
elements.formula-2411-005: \begin{equation}\frac{ d\sigma }{ dt } ( ab \rightarrow cd; s, t ) = \frac{ \pi \alpha_s^2 }{ s^2 } | \overline{  M  } |^2,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-085: As a good approximation at the high-energy limit, in old versions of PACIAE, quarks (antiquarks) are treated as massless.
elements.sent-2411-086: Thus one has the relation $ s + t + u = 0 $ of the Mandelstam variables.
elements.sent-2411-087: The involved processes and corresponding matrix elements are listed in Table~\ref{tab:massless_process , where `1' and `2' indicate different flavors.}
elements.sent-2411-088: In the listed matrix elements, the second forms are obtained by the small angle approximation.
elements.figure-2411-002: \caption{Matrix elements for the massless $ 2 \rightarrow 2 $ parton-parton scattering processes.} \setlength{\tabcolsep}{0.06\textwidth} \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{2} \begin{tabular}{ c | c | c } Order Process $ | \overline{ \mathcal{ M } } |^2 $ 1 \makecell{ $ q_1 q_2 \rightarrow q_1 q_2 $ $ \bar{ q_1 } \bar{ q_2 } \rightarrow \bar{ q_1 } \bar{ q_2 } $ } $ \frac{4}{9} \frac{ s^2 + u^2 }{ t^2 } \approx \frac{8}{9} \frac{ s^2 }{ t^2 } $ 2 $ q_1 q_1 \rightarrow q_1 q_1 $ $ \frac{4}{9} ( \frac{ s^2 + u^2 }{ t^2 } + \frac{ s^2 + t^2 }{ u^2 }) - \frac{8}{27} \frac{ s^2 }{ ut } \approx \frac{8}{9} \frac{ s^2 }{ t^2 } $ 3 $ q_1 \bar{q_2} \rightarrow q_1 \bar{ q_2} $ $ \frac{4}{9} \frac{ s^2 + u^2 }{ t^2 } \approx \frac{8}{9} \frac{ s^2 }{ t^2 } $ 4 $ q_1 \bar{ q_1 } \rightarrow q_2 \bar{ q_2 } $ $ \frac{4}{9} \frac{ t^2 + u^2 }{ s^2 } = \frac{4}{9} \frac{ t^2 + (t + s)^2 }{ s^2 } $ 5 $ q_1 \bar{ q_1 } \rightarrow q_1 \bar{ q_1 } $ $ \frac{4}{9} ( \frac{ s^2 + u^2 }{ t^2 } + \frac{ t^2 + u^2 }{ s^2 } ) - \frac{8}{27} \frac{ u^2 }{ t s } \approx \frac{8}{9} \frac{ s^2 }{ t^2 }$ 6 $ q \bar{ q } \rightarrow g g $ $ \frac{32}{27} \frac{ u^2 + t^2}{ u t } - \frac{8}{3} \frac{ u^2 + t^2 }{ s^2 } \approx -\frac{32}{27} \frac{ s }{ t } $ 7 $ g g \rightarrow q \bar{ q }$ $ \frac{1}{6} \frac{ u^2 + t^2 }{ u t } - \frac{3}{8} \frac{ u^2 + t^2 }{ s^2 } \approx -\frac{1}{3} \frac{ s }{ t } $ 8 \makecell{ $ q g \rightarrow q g $ $ \bar{q} g \rightarrow \bar{q} g $ } $ - \frac{4}{9} \frac{ u^2 + s^2 }{ u s } + \frac{ u^2 + s^2 }{ t^2 } \approx \frac{ 2 s^2 }{ t^2 } $ 9 $ g g \rightarrow g g $ $ \frac{9}{2} ( 3 - \frac{ u t }{ s^2 } - \frac{ u s }{ t^2 } - \frac{ s t }{ u^2 } ) \approx \frac{9}{2} \frac{ s^2 }{ t^2 } $ \end{tabular} \label{tab:massless_process}
elements.sent-2411-089: The corresponding integral cross section can be calculated as \begin{equation}\sigma( a b \rightarrow c d; s ) = \int_{ -s }^0 \frac{ d\sigma }{ dt } ( a b \rightarrow c d; s, t ) dt. \label{eq:integral_xsect}\end{equation} In addition, the singularities appearing at $ t \rightarrow 0 $ can be regulated by introducing a cutoff of the Debye screening coefficient $ \mu $~\cite{Lei:2023srp,Zhang:1997ej .}
elements.formula-2411-006: \begin{equation}\sigma( a b \rightarrow c d; s ) = \int_{ -s }^0 \frac{ d\sigma }{ dt } ( a b \rightarrow c d; s, t ) dt. \label{eq:integral_xsect}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-090: Mathematically, the integral cross section evaluated using the cutoff regulator is equivalent to changing the lower and upper integral limits in Eq.~(\ref{eq:integral_xsect ): \begin{equation}\sigma( a b \rightarrow c d; s ) = \int_{ -s - \mu^2 }^{ - \mu^2 } \frac{ d\sigma }{ dt } ( a b \rightarrow c d; s, t ) dt. \label{eq:integral_xsect_mu}\end{equation} Furthermore, to expand our model to the heavy quark sector and achieve more accurate simulation, we consider the mass correction to the cross sections of heavy quarks in PACIAE 4.0.}
elements.formula-2411-007: \begin{equation}\sigma( a b \rightarrow c d; s ) = \int_{ -s - \mu^2 }^{ - \mu^2 } \frac{ d\sigma }{ dt } ( a b \rightarrow c d; s, t ) dt. \label{eq:integral_xsect_mu}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-092: We neglect the scatterings between two heavy quarks and the thermal production of heavy quarks at present.
elements.sent-2411-093: When jet partons traverse the medium, they might lose energies, i.e. so-called jet quenching phenomenon~\cite{Bjorken:1982tu,Gyulassy:1990ye .}
elements.sent-2411-094: The part due to the collisions between jet partons and medium partons is denoted as the collisional energy loss.
elements.sent-2411-095: We encode it in the parton-parton rescattering as described in Sec.~\ref{subsubsec:parton_rescattering .}
elements.sent-2411-096: Another important source of the energy loss comes from the medium-induced radiation, i.e. radiative energy loss.
elements.sent-2411-097: In PACIAE, we implement preliminarily the medium-induced radiation using the time-like shower approach.
elements.sent-2411-098: The simple collinear parton splitting~\cite{Webber:1986mc is employed.}
elements.sent-2411-099: We assume the outgoing final states from a parton-parton rescattering would radiate one or more partons with QCD branchings $ q \rightarrow q g $, $ g \rightarrow g g $ and $ g \rightarrow q \bar{q $.}
elements.sent-2411-100: The non-branching probability is determined by the Sudakov form factor~\cite{Sudakov:1954sw .}
elements.sent-2411-101: The Altarelli–Parisi (AP) splitting functions~\cite{Altarelli:1977zs are utilized as splitting kernels.}
elements.sent-2411-102: Whereas the collinear and infrared singularities are regularized by some scale cutoffs.
elements.sent-2411-103: Because of the sophistication, it is still under development.
elements.sent-2411-104: We leave this part for the next work.
elements.sent-2411-105: PACIAE 4.0 provides two different hadronization mechanisms: the string fragmentation model and the coalescence model.
elements.sent-2411-106: Recently, the hybrid hadronization mechanisms of fragmentation + coalescence~\cite{Minissale:2020bif,Plumari:2017ntm,Song:2015sfa,Song:2015ykw,He:2011qa,He:2019vgs,Zhao:2023nrz , even hydro-freeze-out + fragmentation + coalescence~\cite{Zhao:2020wcd,YuanyuanWang:2023wbz have been progressively proposed and successfully predict the experimental data, especially in the heavy quark sector.}}
elements.sent-2411-107: These mechanisms have not yet been considered in PACIAE.
elements.sent-2411-108: Still, we leave them to the next version.
elements.sent-2411-109: In the present version, the fragmentation and the coalescence are separated.
elements.sent-2411-110: After the parton cascade evolution, the diquarks will be recovered in order to preserve the basic baryon number conservation.
elements.sent-2411-111: Partons are also recovered back to the strings (including the junction structure~\cite{Sjostrand:2006za,Bierlich:2022pfr , a complicated string topology and another source of the baryon number conservation).}
elements.sent-2411-112: Then the parton strings will be fed back to PYTHIA and be hadronized into colorless hadrons using the Lund string fragmentation regime.
elements.sent-2411-113: An alternative is the coalescence hadronization model.
elements.sent-2411-114: As a first step, PACIAE will split gluons to $q \bar{q}$ pairs.
elements.sent-2411-115: In the improved coalescence model, a gluon is assumed to be split to a $q \bar{q}$ pair sharing the forward light-cone momentum $ E + p_z $ of the gluon according to the AP splitting function~\cite{Altarelli:1977zs \begin{equation}f(z)_{ g \rightarrow q \bar{ q } } \propto z^2 + (1 - z)^2, \label{AP_split_function}\end{equation} where $ z $ and $ 1 - z $ are the light-cone momentum fraction taken by the quark and the antiquark from the mother gluon, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-116: Additionally, the split $ q $ and $ \bar{q $ acquire locally compensated transverse momenta \begin{equation}\begin{aligned} p_{x}^{q} = -p_{x}^{ \bar{q} }, p_{y}^{q} = -p_{y}^{ \bar{q} }, \end{aligned}\end{equation} differing from the collinear splitting.}
elements.sent-2411-117: A further treatment is the energetic quark (antiquark) deexcitation.
elements.sent-2411-118: We assume an energetic quark (antiquark) excites new $ q \bar{ q $ pairs from the vacuum iteratively.}
elements.sent-2411-119: The transverse momenta of $ q $ and $ \bar{q $ are also sampled from Gaussian distributions compensatively.}
elements.sent-2411-120: For the longitudinal direction, the $ q \bar{ q $ takes a fraction of the forward light-cone momentum from its mother quark (antiquark) $ q_0 $ according to a Lund-form~\cite{Sjostrand:2006za deexcitation function \begin{equation}f(z)_{q_0 \rightarrow q\bar{q} + q_0} \propto z^{-1}(1 - z)^a \exp{ (-b m_T^2 / z) },\end{equation} where $ a $ and $ b $ are two free parameters.}}
elements.formula-2411-010: \begin{equation}f(z)_{q_0 \rightarrow q\bar{q} + q_0} \propto z^{-1}(1 - z)^a \exp{ (-b m_T^2 / z) },\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-121: The quarks and antiquarks then coalesce into hadrons using a simple phenomenological coalescence model~\cite{Lei:2023srp .}
elements.sent-2411-122: One can demand the phase-space constraint \begin{equation}\frac{ 16 \pi^2 }{ 9 } (\Delta r)^3 (\Delta p)^3 = \frac{ h^3 }{ d },\end{equation} where $h^3/d$ is the volume occupied by a single hadron in the phase space, $d$=4 refers to the spin and parity degeneracies of the hadron.
elements.sent-2411-123: For partons that fail to coalesce with the constraint, they will be forced to coalesce into hadrons without phase-space constraint in order to satisfy the flavor conservation at the end of coalescence.
elements.sent-2411-124: PACIAE 4.0 carries hundred of inelastic reaction channels of light hadrons developed from the original LUCIAE~\cite{Andersson:1996xi,Sa:1995fj,Tai:1998hc and old versions of PACIAE~\cite{Sa:2011ye,Sa:2012hb,Zhou:2014qpa,Yan:2018mpp,She:2022vco,Yan:2022byw,Lei:2023srp .}}
elements.sent-2411-125: In this new version, we include more inelastic reactions involving the heavy hadrons: \begin{equation}\begin{aligned} \pi N \rightleftharpoons \pi \Delta, \quad \pi N \rightleftharpoons \rho N, N N \rightleftharpoons N \Delta, \quad \pi \pi \rightleftharpoons K \bar{K}, \pi N \rightleftharpoons K Y, \quad \pi \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{Y}, \pi Y \rightleftharpoons K \Xi, \quad \pi \bar Y \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{\Xi}, \bar K N \rightleftharpoons \pi Y, \quad K \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons \pi \bar{Y}, \bar{K} Y \rightleftharpoons \pi \Xi, \quad K \bar{Y} \rightleftharpoons \pi \bar{\Xi}, \bar{K} N \rightleftharpoons K \Xi, \quad K \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{\Xi}, \pi \Xi \rightleftharpoons K \Omega, \quad \pi \bar{\Xi} \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{\Omega}, K \bar{\Xi} \rightleftharpoons \pi \bar{\Omega}, \quad \bar{K} \Xi \rightleftharpoons \pi \Omega, N \bar{N} ~\text{annihilation}, \quad N \bar{Y} ~\text{annihilation}, J/\psi + N \rightleftharpoons Y_c + \bar{D}, \quad \psi' + N \rightleftharpoons Y_c + \bar{D}, J/\psi + \pi \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D^*}, \quad \psi' + \pi \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D^*}, J/\psi + \rho \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D}, \quad \psi' + \rho \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D}, D + \pi \rightleftharpoons D^* + \rho, \quad D^* + \pi \rightleftharpoons D + \rho, D + N \rightleftharpoons D^* + N, \quad D^* + \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{N}, \end{aligned}\end{equation} where $ Y $ stands for $ \Lambda $ or $ \Sigma $, and $ Y_c $ stands for $ \Lambda_c $ or $ \Sigma_c $.
elements.formula-2411-012: \begin{equation} \pi N \rightleftharpoons \pi \Delta, \quad \pi N \rightleftharpoons \rho N, N N \rightleftharpoons N \Delta, \quad \pi \pi \rightleftharpoons K \bar{K}, \pi N \rightleftharpoons K Y, \quad \pi \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{Y}, \pi Y \rightleftharpoons K \Xi, \quad \pi \bar Y \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{\Xi}, \bar K N \rightleftharpoons \pi Y, \quad K \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons \pi \bar{Y}, \bar{K} Y \rightleftharpoons \pi \Xi, \quad K \bar{Y} \rightleftharpoons \pi \bar{\Xi}, \bar{K} N \rightleftharpoons K \Xi, \quad K \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{\Xi}, \pi \Xi \rightleftharpoons K \Omega, \quad \pi \bar{\Xi} \rightleftharpoons \bar{K} \bar{\Omega}, K \bar{\Xi} \rightleftharpoons \pi \bar{\Omega}, \quad \bar{K} \Xi \rightleftharpoons \pi \Omega, N \bar{N} ~annihilation, \quad N \bar{Y} ~annihilation, J/\psi + N \rightleftharpoons Y_c + \bar{D}, \quad \psi' + N \rightleftharpoons Y_c + \bar{D}, J/\psi + \pi \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D^*}, \quad \psi' + \pi \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D^*}, J/\psi + \rho \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D}, \quad \psi' + \rho \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{D}, D + \pi \rightleftharpoons D^* + \rho, \quad D^* + \pi \rightleftharpoons D + \rho, D + N \rightleftharpoons D^* + N, \quad D^* + \bar{N} \rightleftharpoons D + \bar{N}, \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-126: The experimental data inspired parameterization of total cross sections are adopted~\cite{Koch:1986ud,Schopper:1988hwx .}
elements.sent-2411-127: For the channels with missing data, the total cross sections are assumed as constant values and/or calculated using the additive quark model (AQM)~\cite{Bierlich:2022pfr,Levin:1965mi,Lipkin:1973nt \begin{equation}\begin{aligned} \sigma_{AQM}^{h_1 h_2} = (40~\text{mb}) \frac{n_{eff}^{h_1}}{3} \frac{n_{eff}^{h_2}}{3}, n_{eff} = n_d + n_u + 0.6 n_s + 0.2 n_c + 0.07 n_b. \end{aligned} \label{eq:AQM}\end{equation} In Eq.~(\ref{eq:AQM ), $ h $ denotes the hadron.}}
elements.formula-2411-013: \begin{equation} \sigma_{AQM}^{h_1 h_2} = (40~mb) \frac{n_{eff}^{h_1}}{3} \frac{n_{eff}^{h_2}}{3}, n_{eff} = n_d + n_u + 0.6 n_s + 0.2 n_c + 0.07 n_b.  \label{eq:AQM}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-128: By default, only the particles with the mean proper lifetime greater than 1 cm/$ c = 10^{13 $ fm/$c$~\cite{ALICE:2017hcy are recognized as stable in PACIAE 4.0.}}
elements.sent-2411-129: For simplification, we assume that all the unstable particles produced through the hadronic rescattering process will survive until the end of the hadronic rescattering.
elements.sent-2411-130: Of course, it is easy to switch on the decay of unstable particles in the transport process.
elements.sent-2411-131: So far, the decay of the unstable particles is handled by PYTHIA 6~\cite{Sjostrand:2006za .}
elements.sent-2411-132: In the next version, we will plug in the PYTHIA 8 decayer~\cite{Bierlich:2022pfr .}
elements.sent-2411-133: We have upgraded the Parton And-hadron China Institute Atomic Energy (PACIAE) model to a new version PACIAE 4.0, which is already available to the public.
elements.sent-2411-134: The program has been refactored and rewritten by the free-format modern Fortran mixed with C++ languages instead of the fixed-format FORTRAN 77.
elements.sent-2411-135: This is the first version of PACIAE model transformed from Fortran to C++.
elements.sent-2411-136: The interface to PYTHIA 8.3 has been provided.
elements.sent-2411-137: PACIAE 4.0 now can connect to both Fortran-based PYTHIA 6.4 and C++-based PYTHIA 8.3.
elements.sent-2411-138: Several improvements have also been introduced to model the high-energy collisions.
elements.sent-2411-139: This work is supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China under grant Nos.
